title: Create TDummyFirestoreApi skeleton with constructor, init pipeline, helpers, dispose
blocks: Every downstream task that adds overridden reads, writes, streams, batch, transaction, or query handling.

status_instructions.on_followup: If you decided any helper signature differently than the spec (e.g. `_generateDocJson` returns something else), append to every downstream task's `side_context`.

description: Create `lib/apis/t_dummy_firestore_api.dart` with `class TDummyFirestoreApi<T> extends TFirestoreApi<T>`. This task only delivers the skeleton: constructor (forwarding super params plus dummy-only params), private state (schema, registry, raw json store, stream controller registries, seeded Random), init pipeline (probe → build registry), internal helpers (`_applyLatency`, `_rollFailureException`, `_generateDocJson`, `_materialise`, `_applyTimestamps`, `_genDummyId`, `_emitCollections`, `_emitDoc`, `_pathSnapshot`), and `dispose`. No read/write/stream/batch/transaction overrides yet — those land in subsequent tasks.
context_for_junior: This is the single largest file in the feature and every downstream task edits it, so getting the skeleton right matters. You are building a subclass of TFirestoreApi<T> that will later pretend to be a real Firestore but actually serves from a `Map<String, Map<String, dynamic>>` in memory. In this task you do not override any inherited method — you only set up the scaffolding so the next agents can drop their overrides in without rearranging anything. Read the spec's `dummy_api_class` section top to bottom before you start; it lists every private field, every helper, the init order, and the expected section-header order that dev-conventions/conventions/class-structure.md enforces.
why_it_matters: The constructor must accept the exact super parameters of the real api plus seven dummy-only parameters. Any mismatch means callers cannot swap the api with a single class-name change, which is the entire point of the feature.
instructions: Create `lib/apis/t_dummy_firestore_api.dart` with `class TDummyFirestoreApi<T> extends TFirestoreApi<T>`. Include `part 't_dummy_probing_map_part.dart';` or whatever wiring the previous task's output requires; read the probing map task's `worklog` before deciding.

Constructor forwards every super param listed in `dummy_api_class.constructor.super_parameters_forwarded_unchanged` and accepts every dummy-only param listed in `dummy_api_class.constructor.dummy_only_parameters` with the defaults the spec gives. Add an `assert(fromJson != null)` at the top because the probe pass requires it.
Capture `_pathSnapshot` as the result of calling `collectionPath()` once at construction time per the spec's `collection_path_snapshot` rule.
Construct the seeded `Random` from the `seed` parameter, falling back to `_pathSnapshot.hashCode` when the caller did not supply one.

Run the init pipeline in the order the spec lists: `_schema ← _TDummyProbingMap.probe<T>(fromJson)`; `_registry ← TValueGeneratorRegistry.build(fieldGenerators: fieldGenerators, typeGenerators: typeGenerators, schema: _schema, random: _rng)`; initialise empty store + stream controller registries.

Implement the internal helpers per the spec's `internal_helpers` list. Keep them private. `_generateDocJson` walks the schema depth-first — for a `TDummySchemaBranch`, recurse into the nested schema and build a nested map; for a `TDummySchemaLeaf`, call `_registry.generate`. Ensure the `idFieldName` is injected with the supplied id.
`_applyLatency` awaits either `dummyDelayDuration` or a uniform-random sample inside `dummyDelayRange` (when non-null) drawn from `_rng`.

`_rollFailureException` picks one subtype from the spec's failure pool using `_rng`, populating it with `path = _pathSnapshot`, the given `operationType`, id, and documentData. Return the exception when the roll trips, return null otherwise. Read `lib/exceptions/t_firestore_exception.dart` to find the constructors for the six subclasses.
`_applyTimestamps` mirrors `TTimestampType.add` semantics from the real api — reuse the same enum from the existing `turbo_firestore_api` package; do not rewrite the logic.
`_genDummyId` returns a deterministic id of the shape `'dummy_' + _pathSnapshot + '_' + count.toString()` where `count` is an internal monotonic counter.

`_emitCollections` walks `_collectionControllers` and calls `ctrl.add(_store.values.map(_materialise).toList(growable: false))` on each. `_emitDoc(id, entity)` walks `_docControllers[id] ?? {}` and calls `ctrl.add(entity)`.
`dispose` closes every controller in both registries then calls `super.dispose()`.

Keep section headers in the class body in this order: constructor → DEPENDENCIES → STATE → UTIL → FETCHERS → HELPERS → MUTATORS → dispose. Downstream tasks will slot their overrides into FETCHERS / MUTATORS.

Run `make analyze package=turbo_firestore_api` and the new unit tests. No read/write/stream overrides in this task means the analyzer will not complain about missing overrides because the parent class is non-abstract.
goals: A compile-clean, analyze-clean, skeleton class that downstream tasks can extend without structural edits.
Latency and failure helpers ready for the first override task to call into.
non_goals: No read/write/stream overrides in this task.
No behavioural test coverage of Firestore semantics — those tests ship with the tasks that add the overrides.
acceptance_criteria: `lib/apis/t_dummy_firestore_api.dart` exists, extends `TFirestoreApi<T>`, compiles, and analyzes clean.
Constructor accepts every parameter listed in the spec under `dummy_api_class.constructor` with correct defaults.

Unit tests prove: (a) constructor probes the schema and builds a registry, (b) `_genDummyId` is deterministic across two instances with the same seed + path, (c) `_applyLatency` completes within `dummyDelayDuration ± 10%` for the fixed case, (d) `_rollFailureException` returns null when `randomFailurePercentage = 0` and returns a non-null `TFirestoreException` subtype when `randomFailurePercentage = 100`.
Dispose closes all controllers without leaking.
constraints: No fake Firestore layer — this class simply does not touch `_firebaseFirestore` except by forwarding it to super.
No workaround for the `Random` — determinism is mandatory.
Do not write to or import anything from turbo_template or sibling packages.
tools: RepoPrompt MCP for reading t_firestore_api.dart, t_firestore_exception.dart, TTimestampType, and any existing helpers you need to match

testing.rules: No mocks on the dummy api itself. You may construct a real `FirebaseFirestore.instance` only if absolutely necessary; otherwise, wire up a fake Firestore via the existing fake_cloud_firestore test dependency if already present in the package, or construct the dummy api with `firebaseFirestore` as the Fake from `fake_cloud_firestore`. Check `turbo_firestore_api/pubspec.yaml` dev_dependencies first. If no fake is available, pass a real `FirebaseFirestore.instance` and rely on the dummy never touching it (the spec guarantees this).
scenarios: Given a synthetic DTO, When a TDummyFirestoreApi<T> is constructed, Then `_schema` matches what `_TDummyProbingMap.probe<T>` produces standalone.
Given two TDummyFirestoreApi<T> instances with the same seed and same collectionPath, When `_genDummyId` is called ten times on each, Then both instances produce identical id lists.
Given `randomFailurePercentage = 0`, When `_rollFailureException` runs 100 times, Then it returns null on every call.
Given `randomFailurePercentage = 100`, When `_rollFailureException` runs 100 times, Then it returns a `TFirestoreException` subtype on every call and the subtypes vary across the pool.
Given `dummyDelayDuration = Duration(milliseconds: 50)`, When `_applyLatency` is awaited, Then the elapsed duration is within [45ms, 75ms].
Given an instance with open controllers, When dispose is called, Then the isClosed flag on every controller is true.
testing.refactor_for_testability: If a helper is hard to test because it is inlined into the constructor, hoist it to a package-private method exposed via an `@visibleForTesting` annotation from `package:meta`.

quality_bar.maintainability: Every private helper has a single-sentence dartdoc explaining its contract.
quality_bar.dry: No copy-pasted timestamp or path logic — reuse the existing TTimestampType from the real api.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: Skeleton owns wiring only; behaviour lives in the override tasks.
quality_bar.performance: Init pipeline runs once per instance; store and controller maps grow O(N) with store size.
quality_bar.abuse_resistance: `assert(fromJson != null)` stops constructor misuse at development time. Invalid `randomFailurePercentage` (outside 0..100) throws ArgumentError at construction.
